sections.label: Section 204
sections.translation: And then you agreed to take the fresh blood on yourself .

sections.label: Section 205
sections.translation: And I ordered him to turn for expiation to this house .

sections.label: Section 206
sections.original: καὶ τὰς προπομποὺς δῆτα τάσδε λοιδορεῖς ;
sections.translation: And do you then rebuke us , the ones who escorted him here ?

sections.label: Section 207
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ δόμοισι τοῖσδε πρόσφορον μολεῖν .
sections.translation: Yes , for you are not fit to approach this house .

sections.label: Section 208
sections.translation: But this has been assigned to us —

sections.label: Section 209
sections.original: τίς ἥδε τιμή ; κόμπασον γέρας καλόν .
sections.translation: What is this office of yours ? Boast of your fine privilege !

sections.label: Section 210
sections.translation: We drive matricides from their homes .

sections.label: Section 211
sections.original: τί γάρ ; γυναικὸς ἥτις ἄνδρα νοσφίσῃ ;
sections.translation: But what about a wife who kills her husband ?

sections.label: Section 212
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν γένοιθ ὅμαιμος αὐθέντης φόνος .
sections.translation: That would not be murder of a relative by blood .

sections.label: Section 213
sections.original: ἦ κάρτ ἄτιμα , καὶ παρ οὐδὲν εἰργάσω [*]
sections.translation: Then truly you dishonor and bring to nothing the pledges of Hera , the Fulfiller , and Zeus . [*]

sections.label: Section 215
sections.original: Κύπρις δ ἄτιμος τῷδ ἀπέρριπται λόγῳ ,
sections.translation: Cypris too is cast aside , dishonored by this argument , and from her come the dearest things for mortals . For marriage ordained by fate for a man and a woman is greater than an oath and guarded by Justice . If , then , one should kill the other and you are so lenient

sections.label: Section 220
sections.original: τὸ μὴ τίνεσθαι [*] μηδ ἐποπτεύειν κότῳ ,
sections.translation: as not to punish or visit them with anger , I claim that you unjustly banish Orestes from his home . For I see you taking the one cause very much to heart , but clearly acting more leniently about the other . But the goddess Pallas will oversee the pleadings in this case .

sections.label: Section 225
sections.original: τὸν ἄνδρ ἐκεῖνον οὔ τι μὴ λίπω [*] ποτέ .
sections.translation: I will never , never leave that man !

sections.label: Section 226
sections.original: σὺ δ οὖν δίωκε καὶ πόνον πλέω [*] τίθου .
sections.translation: Pursue him then and get more trouble for yourself .

sections.label: Section 227
sections.original: τιμὰς σὺ μὴ ξύντεμνε τὰς ἐμὰς λόγῳ .
sections.translation: Do not cut short my privileges by your words .

sections.label: Section 228
sections.original: οὐδ ἂν δεχοίμην ὥστ ἔχειν τιμὰς σέθεν .
sections.translation: I would not take your privileges as a gift .

sections.label: Section 229
sections.original: μέγας γὰρ ἔμπας πὰρ Διὸς θρόνοις λέγει :
sections.translation: No , for in any case you are called great at the throne of Zeus .

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: ἐγὼ δ , ἄγει [*] γὰρ αἷμα μητρῷον , [*] δίκας
sections.translation: But as for me — since a mother ’ s blood leads me , I will pursue my case against this man and I will hunt him down . Exeunt .

sections.label: Section 232
sections.original: ἐγὼ δ ἀρήξω τὸν ἱκέτην τε ῥύσομαι :
sections.translation: And I will aid my suppliant and rescue him ! For the wrath of the one who seeks purification is terrible among mortals and gods , if I intentionally abandon him . Enters the Sanctuary .

sections.label: Section 235
sections.translation: Lady Athena , at Loxias ’ command I have come . Receive kindly an accursed wretch , not one who seeks purification , or with unclean hand , but with my guilt ’ s edge already blunted and worn away at other homes and in the travelled paths of men .

sections.label: Section 240
sections.translation: Going over land and sea alike , keeping the commands of Loxias ’ oracle , I now approach your house and image , goddess . Here I will keep watch and await the result of my trial .

sections.label: Section 245
sections.translation: Aha ! This is a clear sign of the man . Follow the hints of a voiceless informer . For as a hound tracks a wounded fawn , so we track him by the drops of blood . My lungs pant from many tiring struggles , for I have roamed over the whole earth ,

sections.label: Section 250
sections.translation: and I have come over the sea in wingless flight , pursuing him , no slower than a ship . And now he is here somewhere , cowering . The smell of human blood gives me a smiling welcome .

sections.label: Section 254
sections.translation: Look ! Look again !

sections.label: Section 255
sections.translation:
Look everywhere , so that the matricide will not escape by secret flight , with his debt unpaid !
Yes , here he is again with a defense ; his arms twisted around the image of the immortal goddess ,

sections.label: Section 260
sections.translation:
he wishes to be tried for his debt . [*]
But that is not possible ; a mother ’ s blood upon the earth is hard to recover — alas , the liquid poured on the ground is gone .
But you must allow me in return to suck

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: ἐρυθρὸν ἐκ μελῶν [*] πέλανον : ἀπὸ δὲ σοῦ
sections.translation:
the red blood from your living limbs . May I feed on you — a gruesome drink !
I will wither you alive and drag you down , so that you pay atonement for your murdered mother ’ s agony .
And you will see any other mortal who has sinned by not honoring

sections.label: Section 270
sections.translation:
a god or a stranger or dear parents , each having a just punishment .
For Hades is mighty in holding mortals to account under the earth ,

sections.label: Section 275
sections.translation: and he observes all things and within his mind inscribes them .

sections.label: Section 276
sections.translation: Taught by misery , I know many purification rituals , and I know where it is right to speak and equally to be silent ; and in this case , I have been ordered to speak by a wise teacher .

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: βρίζει γὰρ αἷμα καὶ μαραίνεται χερός ,
sections.translation: For the blood is slumbering and fading from my hand , the pollution of matricide is washed away ; while it was still fresh , it was driven away at the hearth of the god Phoebus by purifying sacrifices of swine . It would be a long story to tell from the beginning ,

sections.label: Section 285
sections.translation:
how many people I have visited , with no harm from association with me . Time purges all things , aging with them .
So now with a pure mouth I piously invoke Athena , lady of this land , to come to my aid . Without the spear ,

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: αὐτόν τε καὶ γῆν καὶ τὸν Ἀργεῖον λεὼν
sections.translation: she will win me and my land and the Argive people as faithful and true allies for all time . But whether in some region of the Libyan land , near the waters of Triton , her native stream , she is in action or at rest , [*]

sections.label: Section 295
sections.translation: aiding those whom she loves , or whether , like a bold marshal , she is surveying the Phlegraean [*] plain , oh , let her come — as a goddess , she hears even from far away — to be my deliverer from distress !

sections.label: Section 299
sections.original: οὔτοι σ Ἀπόλλων οὐδ Ἀθηναίας σθένος
sections.translation: No , neither Apollo nor Athena ’ s strength

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: ῥύσαιτ ἂν ὥστε μὴ οὐ παρημελημένον
sections.translation:
can save you from perishing abandoned , not knowing where joy is in your heart — a bloodless victim of the gods below , a shadow .
You do not answer , but scorn my words , you who are fattened and consecrated to me ?

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: καὶ ζῶν με δαίσεις οὐδὲ πρὸς βωμῷ σφαγείς :
sections.translation: Living , you will be my feast , not slain at an altar ; now you will hear this hymn , a spell to bind you .

sections.label: Section 307
sections.original: ἄγε δὴ καὶ χορὸν ἅψωμεν , ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: Come now , let us also join the dance , since we are resolved to display our hated song

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: λέξαι τε λάχη τὰ κατ ἀνθρώπους
sections.translation: and to declare our allotted office , how our party directs the affairs of men . We claim to be just and upright . No wrath from us will come stealthily to the one who holds out clean hands ,

sections.label: Section 315
sections.translation: and he will go through life unharmed ; but whoever sins , as this man has , and hides his blood-stained hands , as avengers of bloodshed we appear against him to the end , presenting ourselves as upright witnesses for the dead .

sections.label: Section 1.321
sections.original: μᾶτερ ἅ μ ἔτικτες , ὦ μᾶτερ
sections.translation: O mother Night , hear me , mother who gave birth to me as a retribution for the blind and the seeing . For Leto ’ s son dishonors me

sections.label: Section 1.325
sections.translation: by snatching away this cowering wretch , a proper expiation for his mother ’ s blood .

sections.label: Section 1.328
sections.translation: This is our song over the sacrificial victim — frenzied ,

sections.label: Section 1.330
sections.translation: maddened , destroying the mind , the Furies ’ hymn , a spell to bind the soul , not tuned to the lyre , withering the life of mortals .

sections.label: Section 1.334
sections.translation: For this is the office that relentless

sections.label: Section 1.335
sections.translation: Fate spun for us to hold securely : when rash murders of kin come upon mortals , we pursue them until they go under the earth ; and after death ,

sections.label: Section 1.340
sections.translation: they have no great freedom .

sections.label: Section 1.341
sections.translation: This is our song over the sacrificial victim — frenzied , maddened , destroying the mind , the Furies ’ hymn ,

sections.label: Section 1.345
sections.translation: a spell to bind the soul , not tuned to the lyre , withering the life of mortals .

sections.label: Section 2.347
sections.original: γιγνομέναισι λάχη τάδ ἐφ ἁμὶν ἐκράνθη ,
sections.translation: This office was ordained for us at birth ;

sections.label: Section 2.350
sections.original: ἀθανάτων δ ἀπέχειν χέρας , οὐδέ τις ἐστὶ
sections.translation: but the immortal gods must hold back their hands from us , nor does any of them share a feast in common with us ; and I have neither lot nor portion of pure white ceremonial robes ⋯

sections.label: Section 2.354
sections.translation: For I have chosen the overthrow of houses ,

sections.label: Section 2.355
sections.translation: whenever violence raised in the home seizes someone near and dear . So speeding after this man , we weaken him , even though he is strong , because of the fresh blood .

sections.label: Section 2.360
sections.translation: We are eager to take these cares away from another , and to establish for the gods exemption from my concerns , so that it will not come to trial ;

sections.label: Section 2.365
sections.translation: for Zeus has considered us , a blood-dripping , hateful band , unworthy of his council .

sections.label: Section 2.368
sections.translation: And men ’ s thoughts , very proud under the sky , waste away and dwindle in dishonor beneath the earth ,

sections.label: Section 2.370
sections.translation: at our attack in black robes and the vindictive dance of our feet .

sections.label: Section 2.372
sections.translation: For surely with a great leap from above I bring down the heavily falling force of my foot ,

sections.label: Section 3.375
sections.translation: my limbs that trip even swift runners — unendurable ruin .

sections.label: Section 3.377
sections.original: πίπτων δ οὐκ οἶδεν τόδ ὑπ ἄφρονι λύμᾳ :
sections.translation: But , as he falls , he does not know it , because of his senseless folly ; pollution hovers over the man in such darkness , and mournful rumor speaks of
